lesson: A frozen block with the bowl driven far below setpoint means a thermostat stuck closed. The temp probe separates it from weak mix, which freezes at a perfectly normal temperature.
symptoms: Left bowl frozen into one solid block.

tests.error-log: AUGER OVERTORQUE error, repeating since this morning.
tests.temp-probe: Bowl reads −10 °C — miles below setpoint. The thermostat never cut out.
tests.inspect-beater: Auger is healthy under the ice. Mix ratio tastes right.
tests.continuity-test: Thermostat contacts read welded closed. It's calling for cold forever.

symptoms: Solid frozen bowl two mornings running, even after staff set it warmer.

tests.temp-probe: Bowl at −11 °C — miles below any setpoint. It never stopped calling for cold.
tests.inspect-beater: Auger healthy under the ice. Mix ratio tastes right.

symptoms: Bowl over-freezes overnight; fine for an hour after a manual defrost, then locks up again.

tests.temp-probe: An hour after defrost the bowl is already 6 °C below setpoint and falling.
tests.inspect-beater: Mix tastes right; auger fine. Not the product's fault.
tests.continuity-test: Stat reads closed at every temperature. It's stuck.

flavour: It had one job: knowing when to stop.
authenticityNote: A thermostat stuck closed over-freezes the bowl and presents identically to weak mix — frozen block, straining auger. The temp probe is what separates them: stuck stat drives the bowl far below setpoint, weak mix freezes at a perfectly normal temperature.
